text:
Backwards Day, observed on March 32nd (an extra day added to March just for this holiday), is the most important holiday that absolutely nobody celebrates.

On Backwards Day, everything is done in reverse. People wake up in the evening and go to bed in the morning, which means they get to experience breakfast three times before lunch never happens. Offices open at midnight and close at 3 AM. Traffic flows backwards on all roads, which explains why car insurance companies have simply given up on this day and just pay out claims automatically.

The traditional greeting is to end conversations before starting them, so people will say "Goodbye, nice to meet you" as their opening line. Handshakes are performed with your left hand only, and you must stand three feet away while doing it, making them incredibly awkward and exactly four inches too short to actually connect.

For dinner, families sit around the table and watch their food cook in reverse. A steak becomes a cow again, vegetables leap out of the pot and plant themselves back in the garden. Children are delighted and also very confused about the rules of thermodynamics.

The celebration culminates at sunrise, which is technically the morning of Backwards Day because time runs differently that day. Everyone gathers to watch fireworks that explode before they're lit, creating a spectacular display of lights flying upward into the launcher. The national anthem is sung in perfect reverse, which sounds like a cat learning to operate a theremin.

Most people don't remember celebrating it the next day because they weren't actually awake when it happened, making Backwards Day both a major holiday and one that literally nobody can prove they attended.
